concepts.title: Definition of Doping
concepts.description: Doping refers to the use of banned substances or methods to enhance athletic performance.

concepts.title: Types of Doping
concepts.description: There are two types of doping: (1) Physical doping (e.g., blood transfusions, anabolic steroids) and (2) Chemical doping (e.g., stimulants, narcotics)

concepts.title: Effects of Doping
concepts.description: Doping can lead to serious health problems, including cardiovascular issues, liver damage, and psychological dependence.

concepts.title: Banned Substances
concepts.description: PAIN - Performance-Enhancing And Narcotics In Sports

concepts.title: Distinguishing Between Therapeutic and Performance-Enhancing Substances
concepts.description: Students often confuse therapeutic substances with performance-enhancing substances. However, therapeutic substances are used to treat medical conditions, whereas performance-enhancing substances are used to gain a competitive advantage.

concepts.title: Consequences of Doping
concepts.description: Doping can result in suspension or lifetime ban from competition, loss of medals or titles, and damage to an athlete's reputation and career.

concepts.title: WADA Code
concepts.description: The World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) Code is the international standard for anti-doping in sports, outlining the rules and procedures for testing and punishment.

concepts.title: Prevention and Education
concepts.description: Preventing doping requires education, awareness, and a strong anti-doping culture within sports organisations and communities.
